meta.title: Lesson 6: 富士山に登りましょう — Grammar
meta.subject: japanese
meta.description: Grammar patterns for Minna no Nihongo Lesson 6: Let's Climb Mt. Fuji

items.back:
でんしゃ (電車) = electric train
バス = bus
ちかてつ (地下鉄) = subway
タクシー = taxi
ひこうき (飛行機) = airplane
ふね (船) = ship
くるま (車) = car
じてんしゃ (自転車) = bicycle

のります (乗ります) = to board/ride
おります (降ります) = to get off
あるきます (歩きます) = to walk
のぼります (登ります) = to climb

で = by (means): でんしゃで = by train
に = onto: でんしゃに乗ります = board the train

〜ませんか = Won't you ~? (inviting someone)
一緒に映画を見ませんか。
= Won't you watch a movie together?

〜ましょう = Let's ~! (agreement/suggestion)
行きましょう！= Let's go!

〜ましょうか = Shall I ~? / Shall we ~?

Offering help:
かばんを持ちましょうか。
= Shall I carry your bag?

Suggesting together:
一緒に行きましょうか。= Shall we go together?

items.front: Soft refusals with ちょっと
items.back:
ちょっと... is used to politely decline invitations
without saying NO directly.

A: 映画を見ませんか。
B: すみません、ちょっと...

A: Won't you watch a movie?
B: I'm sorry, it's a bit... (implying no)

items.front: でから = and then / after doing
items.back:
[Verb て-form]から + [next action]

でんしゃに乗ってから、バスに乗ります。
= After taking the train, I take the bus.

Can also use 〜てから for 'after doing':
食べてから、歯を磨きます。
= After eating, I brush my teeth.

て-form is used to connect actions and make requests.

Group 1 (u-verbs): 〜って / 〜いて / 〜して
飲む → 飲んで
書く → 書いて

Group 2 (ru-verbs): 〜て
食べる → 食べて
見る → 見て

Irregular:
する → して
くる → きて

一緒に〜ませんか。= Won't you ~ together?
いいですね！= That sounds great!
いいですよ。= That's fine / Sure.
すみません、ちょっと... = Sorry, a bit difficult...
また今度。= Another time.
ぜひ！= By all means! / Definitely!

items.question: 一緒に映画を見 ___ か。(Won't you watch a movie with me?)
items.explanation: 〜ませんか = invitation 'Won't you ~?' Polite and indirect way to invite someone.

items.question: A: 行きませんか。B: ___！(Let's go!)
items.explanation: ましょう = Let's ~. Used to accept an invitation or make a suggestion.

items.question: でんしゃ ___ 行きます。(I go by train.)
items.explanation: で marks the means of transport. でんしゃで = by train. Do not confuse with に which marks the destination.

items.question: えき ___ 電車に乗ります。(I board the train at the station.)
items.explanation: で marks the location of an action. えきで = at the station (where the action of boarding happens).

items.question: かばんを持ち ___ か。(Shall I carry your bag?)
items.explanation: 〜ましょうか = Shall I ~? / Shall we ~? Used to offer help or make suggestions.

items.question: A: 映画を見ませんか。B: すみません、___... (soft refusal)
items.explanation: ちょっと... (trailing off) is the Japanese polite way to decline. Saying a direct 'no' can seem rude.

items.question: 食べて ___ 、歯を磨きます。(After eating, I brush my teeth.)
items.explanation: 〜てから = after doing ~. [Verb て-form] + から = after doing that action.

items.question: Which て-form is correct for 飲む (to drink)?
items.explanation: む → んで is one of the て-form rules. む/ぬ/ぶ → んで. So 飲む → 飲んで.

items.question: Which て-form is correct for 書く (to write)?
items.explanation: く → いて is the て-form rule for く-ending verbs. 書く → 書いて. Exception: 行く → 行って (not 行いて)!

items.question: Which て-form is correct for 食べる (to eat)?
items.explanation: Group 2 (ru-verbs): just replace る with て. 食べる → 食べて. Simple!

items.question: How do you say 'by bicycle'?
items.explanation: で marks means of transport. じてんしゃで = by bicycle.

items.question: 一緒に食事しませんか → You want to accept. What do you say?
items.answer: ぜひ！一緒に食事しましょう！
options: ぜひ！一緒に食事しましょう！
items.explanation: ぜひ = by all means / definitely! + ましょう = let's. Express enthusiasm when accepting invitations.

items.question: いっしょに えいがを み _____か。(Shall we watch a movie together?)
items.explanation: 〜ませんか = Shall we ~? / Would you like to ~? (invitation)

items.question: A: 9じに ロビーで あいましょう。B: _____、そうしましょう。
items.explanation: ええ = yes (informal/conversational). そうしましょう = let's do that.

items.question: でんしゃ_____ ひろしままで いきます。(I go to Hiroshima by train.)
items.explanation: で marks means of transport: でんしゃで = by train.

items.question: きっぷを _____ ください。(Please buy a ticket.)
items.explanation: かう → かって (て-form of かう: buy). 〜てください = please do ~.

items.question: バスに _____。(Please get on the bus.)
options: あるいて ください
はいって ください
items.explanation: のります → のって = get on (て-form). のってください = please board.

items.question: くうこうまで どうやって いきますか。——でんしゃ _____ いきます。
items.explanation: Transport+で = by [transport]. でんしゃで = by train.

items.question: タクシー_____ いきましょうか。(Shall we go by taxi?)
items.explanation: タクシーで = by taxi. で = means/method.

items.question: てを _____ から、しょくじしましょう。(After washing hands, let's eat.)
items.explanation: あらう → あらって (て-form: wash hands). て-form connects actions.

items.question: 一緒に映画を見 ___ か。(Won't you watch a movie with me?)
items.explanation: 〜ませんか = invitation 'Won't you ~?' Polite and indirect way to invite someone.

items.question: A: 行きませんか。B: ___！(Let's go!)
items.explanation: ましょう = Let's ~. Used to accept an invitation or make a suggestion.

items.explanation: で marks the means of transport. でんしゃで = by train. Do not confuse with に which marks the destination.

items.question: えき ___ 電車に乗ります。(I board the train at the station.)
items.explanation: で marks the location of an action. えきで = at the station (where the action of boarding happens).

items.question: かばんを持ち ___ か。(Shall I carry your bag?)
items.explanation: 〜ましょうか = Shall I ~? / Shall we ~? Used to offer help or make suggestions.

items.explanation: ちょっと... (trailing off) is the Japanese polite way to decline. Saying a direct 'no' can seem rude.

items.answer: ぜひ！一緒に食事しましょう！
options: ぜひ！一緒に食事しましょう！
items.explanation: ぜひ = by all means / definitely! + ましょう = let's. Express enthusiasm when accepting invitations.
